target.name: XMOS
target.founder: Ali Dixon, James Foster, Noel Hurley, David May, Hitesh Mehta

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
XMOS is a growth-stage semiconductor company that has raised £200M+ to date. The peer universe in Chips & Semiconductors comprises 14 same-sector comparables: 3 public (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private firms including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, Etched, SambaNova Systems, and Credo Semiconductor.

Two standard valuation methods were assessed. Peer-Median multiples are inapplicable: while 14 sector peers exist, none yield three or more same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier datapoints, making median derivation unreliable. Public-Comp Anchoring is similarly not executable—the semiconductor sector median P/S is 20.3x across the three public tickers, but XMOS's revenue is not disclosed, preventing application of this multiple.

The absence of a sufficiently dense narrow peer set and missing revenue disclosure means traditional triangulation cannot be performed. Any valuation would rest on bottom-up cost-of-capital methods, management guidance, or transaction precedent from the private comparables—none of which are documented here. Given the stage and sector, investors should prioritize obtaining XMOS revenue and profitability metrics, then benchmarking against the identified private peer cohort (Cerebras, PsiQuantum, SambaNova) on growth trajectory and capital efficiency. Without these inputs, quantitative valuation remains indeterminate.
